m_Item1.QuestionText: A graph with n vertices will definitely have a parallel edge or self loop of the total number of edges are
m_Item1.Explanation: This is because this number represents the maximum possible number of edges in a simple graph without parallel edges or self-loops. Any additional edge beyond this number must result in a parallel edge or a self-loop.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The maximum degree of any vertex in a simple graph with n vertices is
m_Item1.Explanation: In a Graph all vetises may be connected with all the vertices other than itself in the graph. In that case its degree will be n-1.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Time Complexity of DFS is? (V – number of vertices, E – number of edges)
m_Item1.Explanation: The time complexity of DFS if the entire tree is traversed is O(V)O(V) where V is the number of nodes. In the ca  se of a graph, the time complexity is O(V + E)O(V+E) where V is the number of vertexes and E i  s the number of edges.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A person wants to visit some places. He starts from a vertex and then wants to visit every place connected to this vertex and so on. What algorithm he should use?
m_Item1.Explanation: This is the definition of the Breadth First Search. Exploring a node, then it’s neighbors and so on.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Connected graph contains minimum _____ number of edges.
m_Item1.Explanation: Connected Graph means  all the vertices should be connected.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Adjacency List representation of a graph can be implemented by using _______________.
m_Item1.Explanation: An adjacency list represents a graph as an array of linked lists. The index of the array represents a vertex and each element in its linked list represents the other vertices that form an edge with the vertex.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Subgraph of a graph can be formed by removing one or more edges from it in such a way that it remains connected and
do not contains a cycle is reffered as _______________.
m_Item1.Explanation:
A spanning tree is a subset of Graph G, which has all the vertices covered with minimum possible number of edges. Hence, a spanning tree does not have cycles and it cannot be disconnected..
By this definition, we can draw a conclusion that every connected and undirected Graph G has at least one spanning tree.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a given graph, if all the vertices are adjacent with  remaininig all vertices then such a graph is reffered as
m_Item1.Explanation:
This is  example of Complete Graph where Vertex A is connected to all remaning vertices [ B,C,D ]
vertex B is connected to all remaning vertieces [ A, C ,D ]
vertex C is connected to all remaning vertieces [ A , B ,D]
vertex D is connected to all remaning vertieces [ A, B ,C ]

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the graph M with 3 vertices. Its adjacency matrix is shown below. Which of the following is true?
​

Options.OptionText: Graph M has no minimum spanning tree
Graph M has a unique minimum spanning trees of cost 2
Graph M has 3 distinct minimum spanning trees, each of cost 2
Graph M has 3 spanning trees of different costs

m_Item1.Explanation:
Here all non-diagonal elements in the adjacency matrix are 1. So, every vertex is connected every other vertex of the graph. And, so graph M has 3 distinct minimum
​

m_Item1.QuestionText: A graph is said to be a tree, if it satisfies which of the properties:

Options.OptionText: If it is connected and there are no cycles in the graph.
If it is not connected and there are cycles in the graph
If it connected and there are cycles in the graph

m_Item1.Explanation: A tree is a hierarchical structure with a single root and no cycles, ensuring a unique path between any two nodes. In contrast, a graph is a more flexible representation, allowing for cycles, multiple paths between nodes, and potentially no single root, accommodating complex relationships like networks.
